content:
Hi
The default code usually gives hints on the type of the expected output, but here the stub generator generates code outputting '()' instead of an integer.

We have many clashes about balancing parentheses, but while this one is on the same theme, it actually requires a different task. Good job (unless I've overlooked a similar existing one)!

Goal
* Note that, like [[variables]], constants also have their own formatting syntax: {{constants}}.

For point 1, please format "(" and ")" as {{constants}}.

For point 2, please format "())" and "(()" as {{constants}}.

Input
* Please format "(" and ")" as {{constants}}.

Constraints
* Unless you plan to add cases with longer strings, please reduce the upper bound to 10.

Stub generator
* Please update the value 1000 here if the constraint is revised.

Cases
* The titles should use "unbalanced" instead of "balanced" to correctly describe the cases.

Thanks.
I have updated the contribution. If you have any objections, you can give me some more suggestions.
It's because I wrote it "()". Thanks for reminding me about this.
Thank you for the quick follow-up. I don't have further comments at the moment. 🙂
